metadata.title: Dr. Upham and family planning trip to Japan, 16 November 1937
metadata.transcript:
PAGE TWO

OXFORD

Ralph McGinnis, correspondent, phone Oxford 256-J or 560-L.
Miss Jeni Van Ausdal, society and personals, phone Oxford 596.
News items may be left at Honack's Studio.
J. C. Byrne Drug company, business agent for the Journal-News
Phone Oxford 41 for subscriptions and advertisements.

DR. UPHAM AND FAMILY
PLANNING TRIP TO JAPAN

To Leave San Francisco Next
Week For Voyage; Duties
On Campus Placed In
Hands Of A. K. Morris

Special To The Journal-News.
		Oxford, Nov. 16.
	Dr. A. H. Upham, Miami president, Mrs. Upham, and daughter, Peggy, will leave this week for a winter vacation which will be in the nature of a Sabbatical leave.
	The Uphams plan to leave San Francisco early next week and will visit Japan, Hong Kong, the Philippines, Java, India, Egypt, and the Mediterranean countries, returning via New York.
	Dr. Upham will study educational methods in foreign universities and will visit Miami alumni living abroad.
	They will be met in Honolulu by Mrs. W. H. Shideler, and later will meet Dr. Shideler, who is touring the world, in Bombay.
	The Uphams plan to renew old acquaintances among Miami alumni now living in Japan, the Philippines, and other countries.
	Dr. Upham returned to Miami in 1927 after an absence of eight years as president of the University of Idaho. A. K. Morris, vice president, will act for him in his absence.

Oxford Business Notices

FOR SALE—Electric stove in good condition, Apply McGreevy Dairy and Ice Co., Dixie highway.
79-2t

the American Association for the Advancement of Science to give the guest address at their December meeting in Indianapolis.
	During the speech, Dr. Evans will show 24 reels of motion pictures which he took last summer on his trip in the West. The pictures are on such subjects as the Great Plains, the forests of the Northwest, and the vegetation of the imperial desert.

Dr. Heckert To Head
Red Cross Canvass
	The Red Cross roll-call in Oxford will be conducted by students of Stewart and McGuffey schools. Dr. J. W. Heckert will serve as chairman of the canvass which will be held November 18, 19, and 20 through November 30.

Ticket Sale Meeting
For Turkey
	Seasonal tickets for the Turkey tickets for the Turkey
